content.text: In AutoGen 0.4, the high-level conversation API was redesigned and released as the

and provides ready-made agent types, team patterns, and termination conditions. The 0.4 AgentChat API is async-first and composable — agents and teams communicate via typed messages rather than raw strings, and conversations are driven by a team's

attrs.alt: autogen-agentchat component map: Teams (RoundRobinGroupChat, SelectorGroupChat, Swarm) use Agents (AssistantAgent, CodeExecutorAgent) and TerminationConditions, all built on autogen-core primitives

content.text: LLM-backed agent with optional tool use. The main workhorse.
Executes code blocks produced by other agents. Wraps a code executor (local or Docker).
Relays human input. Used for interactive sessions or injecting human messages.

from autogen_agentchat.agents import AssistantAgent, CodeExecutorAgent
from autogen_agentchat.messages import TextMessage
from autogen_ext.models.openai import OpenAIChatCompletionClient
from autogen_ext.code_executors.local import LocalCommandLineCodeExecutor

model_client = OpenAIChatCompletionClient(model="gpt-4o-mini", api_key="sk-...")

# An LLM agent that can also use tools
assistant = AssistantAgent(
    name="assistant",
    model_client=model_client,
    system_message="You are a helpful Python coding assistant.",
)

# A code executor agent (runs code blocks from the conversation)
code_executor = CodeExecutorAgent(
    name="code_executor",
    code_executor=LocalCommandLineCodeExecutor(work_dir="ws"),
)
. A team manages which agent speaks next and when the conversation ends. Three built-in team types cover the most common patterns:
Agents take turns in the order listed
An LLM model selects the next speaker based on context
Agents hand off explicitly via HandoffMessage

from autogen_agentchat.teams import RoundRobinGroupChat, SelectorGroupChat
from autogen_agentchat.conditions import TextMentionTermination
from autogen_ext.models.openai import OpenAIChatCompletionClient

model_client = OpenAIChatCompletionClient(model="gpt-4o-mini", api_key="sk-...")

# Round-robin: assistant -> code_executor -> assistant -> ...
team_rr = RoundRobinGroupChat(
    participants=[assistant, code_executor],
    termination_condition=TextMentionTermination("TERMINATE"),
)

# Selector: an LLM chooses who speaks next
researcher = AssistantAgent("researcher", model_client=model_client,
    system_message="You gather facts.",
    description="Use researcher for factual questions.")
coder = AssistantAgent("coder", model_client=model_client,
    system_message="You write Python code.",
    description="Use coder for coding tasks.")

team_sel = SelectorGroupChat(
    participants=[researcher, coder, code_executor],
    model_client=model_client,          # used for speaker selection
    termination_condition=TextMentionTermination("TERMINATE"),
)
to start a conversation. The method returns a
containing the full message list and the stop reason. The team handles speaker selection, message passing, and termination internally.

import asyncio
from autogen_agentchat.teams import RoundRobinGroupChat
from autogen_agentchat.conditions import TextMentionTermination

async def main():
    team = RoundRobinGroupChat(
        participants=[assistant, code_executor],
        termination_condition=TextMentionTermination("TERMINATE"),
    )

    result = await team.run(
        task="Write and run Python code that prints the squares of 1 to 5."
    )

    for msg in result.messages:
        print(f"[{msg.source}] {str(msg.content)[:100]}")

    print(f"Stop reason: {result.stop_reason}")

asyncio.run(main())
AgentChat 0.4 uses composable termination conditions instead of the callback-based
from older versions. Conditions can be combined with
Any message contains the given text
The conversation has produced n messages
Total token usage exceeds the limit
A HandoffMessage targeting a specific agent is received

from autogen_agentchat.conditions import (
    TextMentionTermination, MaxMessageTermination, StopMessageTermination,
)

# Stop on TERMINATE OR after 20 messages (whichever comes first)
termination = TextMentionTermination("TERMINATE") | MaxMessageTermination(20)

# Stop only when BOTH conditions are met (unusual, but composable)
strict = TextMentionTermination("DONE") & MaxMessageTermination(10)
to receive messages as they are produced rather than waiting for the full conversation to finish. This is useful for streaming responses to a UI or logging in real time.

import asyncio
from autogen_agentchat.teams import RoundRobinGroupChat
from autogen_agentchat.conditions import TextMentionTermination

async def main():
    team = RoundRobinGroupChat(
        participants=[assistant, code_executor],
        termination_condition=TextMentionTermination("TERMINATE"),
    )

    async for event in team.run_stream(
        task="Explain recursion and give a Python example."
    ):
        # Each event is a TaskResult, AgentMessage, or TerminationEvent
        print(type(event).__name__, ":", str(event)[:80])

asyncio.run(main())
to clear the message history and internal state so the team can run a fresh task. This is more efficient than creating a new team object for each task.

import asyncio

async def run_multiple_tasks():
    team = RoundRobinGroupChat(
        participants=[assistant, code_executor],
        termination_condition=TextMentionTermination("TERMINATE"),
    )

    tasks = [
        "Write a Python function to compute factorial.",
        "Write a Python function to check if a number is prime.",
    ]

    for task in tasks:
        result = await team.run(task=task)
        print(f"Task '{task[:40]}' done: {result.stop_reason}")
        await team.reset()   # clear history before next task

asyncio.run(run_multiple_tasks())
Pass a list of callables to
parameter. The agent registers them as function-calling tools automatically — no separate

import asyncio
import json
from autogen_agentchat.agents import AssistantAgent
from autogen_agentchat.teams import RoundRobinGroupChat
from autogen_agentchat.conditions import TextMentionTermination
from autogen_ext.models.openai import OpenAIChatCompletionClient

model_client = OpenAIChatCompletionClient(model="gpt-4o-mini", api_key="sk-...")

def get_weather(city: str) -> str:
    """Return the current weather conditions for a city."""
    return json.dumps({"city": city, "temp_c": 18, "conditions": "partly cloudy"})

def convert_currency(amount: float, from_ccy: str, to_ccy: str) -> str:
    """Convert an amount between two currencies."""
    rates = {"USD": 1.0, "EUR": 0.92, "GBP": 0.79}
    result = amount * rates.get(to_ccy, 1) / rates.get(from_ccy, 1)
    return json.dumps({"result": round(result, 2), "to": to_ccy})

assistant = AssistantAgent(
    name="assistant",
    model_client=model_client,
    tools=[get_weather, convert_currency],   # pass callables directly
    system_message="Answer questions using your tools when needed.",
)

async def main():
    team = RoundRobinGroupChat(
        participants=[assistant],
        termination_condition=TextMentionTermination("TERMINATE"),
    )
    result = await team.run(
        task="What is the weather in Paris, and convert 100 USD to EUR? Then say TERMINATE."
    )
    for msg in result.messages:
        print(f"[{msg.source}] {str(msg.content)[:120]}")

asyncio.run(main())
team type enables explicit agent handoffs. An agent returns a
to delegate control to another agent by name. The swarm routes the conversation to the named target and the original agent goes idle.

import asyncio
from autogen_agentchat.agents import AssistantAgent
from autogen_agentchat.teams import Swarm
from autogen_agentchat.messages import HandoffMessage
from autogen_agentchat.conditions import HandoffTermination, MaxMessageTermination
from autogen_ext.models.openai import OpenAIChatCompletionClient

model_client = OpenAIChatCompletionClient(model="gpt-4o-mini", api_key="sk-...")

triage = AssistantAgent("triage", model_client=model_client,
    handoffs=["billing", "technical"],
    system_message=(
        "You triage customer requests. "
        "If the issue is about billing, hand off to 'billing'. "
        "If technical, hand off to 'technical'."
    ))
billing = AssistantAgent("billing", model_client=model_client,
    system_message="You handle billing enquiries. Resolve and end with TERMINATE.")
technical = AssistantAgent("technical", model_client=model_client,
    system_message="You handle technical issues. Resolve and end with TERMINATE.")

async def main():
    team = Swarm(
        participants=[triage, billing, technical],
        termination_condition=MaxMessageTermination(10),
    )
    result = await team.run(task="My invoice is wrong — I was charged twice.")
    for msg in result.messages:
        print(f"[{msg.source}] {str(msg.content)[:100]}")

asyncio.run(main())
list contains typed message objects. Filter by type to extract specific content — text replies, tool calls, tool results, or stop messages.
The 0.4 AgentChat API has breaking changes from 0.2. The most important differences to be aware of when migrating existing code:
GroupChat / GroupChatManager are gone. Use RoundRobinGroupChat, SelectorGroupChat, or Swarm instead.
initiate_chat() is replaced by await team.run(task=...) which is async and returns TaskResult.
is_termination_msg callback is replaced by composable TerminationCondition objects.
Tool registration via register_for_llm() / register_for_execution() is replaced by the tools= parameter on AssistantAgent.
llm_config dict is replaced by a model_client object from autogen-ext (e.g. OpenAIChatCompletionClient).
code_execution_config dict is replaced by CodeExecutorAgent wrapping a LocalCommandLineCodeExecutor or DockerCommandLineCodeExecutor.
package (which includes AssistantAgent, UserProxyAgent, GroupChat) is still available as
Test teams by injecting mock model clients that return fixed responses. Use
to script exact sequences of agent replies without calling an LLM.

import asyncio
from autogen_agentchat.agents import AssistantAgent
from autogen_agentchat.teams import RoundRobinGroupChat
from autogen_agentchat.conditions import TextMentionTermination

# ReplayChatCompletionClient returns scripted replies in order
try:
    from autogen_ext.models.replay import ReplayChatCompletionClient
    mock_client = ReplayChatCompletionClient(
        [
            "Here is a prime checker: def is_prime(n): ...",
            "The code looks correct. TERMINATE",
        ]
    )
except ImportError:
    mock_client = None   # use real client if replay is not available

async def test_team_terminates():
    if mock_client is None:
        print("Skipping: ReplayChatCompletionClient not available")
        return
    agent = AssistantAgent("agent", model_client=mock_client)
    team = RoundRobinGroupChat(
        participants=[agent],
        termination_condition=TextMentionTermination("TERMINATE"),
    )
    result = await team.run(task="Write a prime number checker.")
    assert result.stop_reason is not None
    print(f"Stopped after {len(result.messages)} messages: {result.stop_reason}")

asyncio.run(test_team_terminates())
autogen-agentchat is the high-level package in AutoGen 0.4+; it provides agents, teams, and termination conditions built on autogen-core.
The three main agents are AssistantAgent (LLM + tools), CodeExecutorAgent (runs code), and UserProxyAgent (human relay).
Teams replace GroupChat: RoundRobinGroupChat (sequential), SelectorGroupChat (LLM-selected speaker), and Swarm (explicit handoff).
Run a team with await team.run(task=...) which returns a TaskResult with all messages and the stop reason.
Termination conditions are composable objects combined with | (any) or & (all) instead of the older is_termination_msg callback.
Use team.run_stream() for incremental message delivery to UIs or logging systems.
Reset a team with await team.reset() to reuse it for multiple independent tasks without recreating the object.
